panel.generateAnim.playerBendDesc: To apply the animation together with Bend, inside for example Left leg, must be Left leg Bend and so with the rest of the arms and legs.
panel.generateAnim.playerHeadPaintDesc: To demonstrate an example of the correct structure, you can generate a player model via HeadPaint.
panel.generateAnim.playerStructureDesc: To use animation for a player, you need to select a group of the player model, inside of which there are: Head, Body, Left leg, Left leg, Right leg, Right hand and Left hand.

panel.headPaint.aboutDesc: Create and edit custom head textures directly in the editor.
panel.headPaint.apiKeyIncorrect: 403. Please check your API KEY, it appears to be incorrect.
panel.headPaint.copySideOfHead: Copy the side of the head
panel.headPaint.drawOnSecondLayerInfo: When enabled, you draw on the outer (second) layer of the head
panel.headPaint.fillWholeSide: Fill the entire side of the head
panel.headPaint.head: Head
panel.headPaint.imageFormatPngOrJpg: The image format should be png or jpg
panel.headPaint.imageMultipleOf8: The width and height of the image must be a multiple of 8
panel.headPaint.imageNotLargerThan128: The image cannot be larger than 128x128
panel.headPaint.imageNotWiderThan64: The image cannot be wider than 64x
panel.headPaint.imageResolutionHead: The image must have a resolution of exactly 64x64
panel.headPaint.imageResolutionLarge: The image must have a resolution of no more than 128x, as well as its resolution must be a multiple of 8
panel.headPaint.mineSkinApiKeyDesc:
For automatic processing of heads, you need to get the API key of the MineSkin service:
Steps to obtain it:
1. Open a MineSkin Account
2. Sign in with your Google Account
3. Go to the Manage API Keys page
4. Click on "+" and create a new key
5. Copy the new key and paste it into the field below:
panel.headPaint.mineSkinUnavailableAfter: Error: MineSkin is unavailable (429/500) after {{maxAttempts}} attempts
panel.headPaint.replacePixelInfo: When enabled, when painting, the new color completely replaces the old color without blending in
panel.headPaint.showGridInfo: When enabled, displays a grid of pixels on heads. Useful when Smart Grid is enabled
panel.headPaint.showShadowsInfo: When disabled, the editor's lighting is uniform, without shadows
panel.headPaint.skinNotFoundByNickname: Skin not found or not an image for nickname: {{nickname}}
panel.headPaint.smartGridInfo: When enabled, the head grid automatically adjusts to the size of the head so that the head pixels are standard size.
panel.headPaint.textureToModelDesc: Create a ready-made player model from heads, as well as a block, slab, wall, chair, create a painting from heads or simply import a head

panel.properties.additionalNbtInfo: Use this field to add a tag to all the elements inside this collection.
panel.properties.backgroundGroupInfo: Enable if this group is only needed when creating a screenshot. It will not be visible in the model page, as well as models within it will not be exported
panel.properties.defaultBrightnessInfo:
Enable if you want to export along with the default values [sky:15,block:0].
This is useful to keep models from turning black when passing through real blocks
panel.properties.name: name
panel.properties.nbtDesc:
Specify NBT in the correct format. Errors can break the command.
If you want to specify a tag, specify it as Tags:["Name"]
panel.properties.nbtErrorDesc: There are errors in your NBT field. Enter the NBT correctly.Check now
panel.properties.physAngularDampingDesc: Rotation damping. Higher values make the object stop spinning faster.
panel.properties.physFrictionDesc: Surface friction. Higher values make the object slide less.
panel.properties.physicsCollisionInfo: Enable this group for collisions during physics simulation.
panel.properties.physicsDesc: These are the physics simulation options for the group. Enable simulation, adjust the settings, and simulate physics in the Animator to record the movements directly into the animation.
panel.properties.physicsSimulationInfo: Enable this option to have the group simulate physics when the timeline starts. When you stop the timeline, the recorded movements will appear in the timeline itself.
panel.properties.physLinearDampingDesc: Movement damping. Higher values make the object lose speed faster.
panel.properties.physRestitutionDesc: Bounce amount. Higher values make impacts more elastic.
panel.properties.projectNbtInfo: The main NBT for the Root Block Display
panel.properties.projectTitleDesc:
That's your project title. By changing the name, you do not create a new project.
To create a new project, select Project File > New
panel.properties.title: Properties
panel.rootAnim.title: Command Animation
panel.screenshot.title: Screenshot
panel.server.aboutDesc: Build alongside others in real time - you can see their models, they can see yours, but each user edits only their own work.
panel.server.chatOnlyDesc: Enable this if you only want to chat and see other players, without seeing their models or showing your own models to others.
panel.server.kicked: You have been kicked from Share Party
panel.server.minecraftModeDesc: Build and destroy objects in the Minecraft style
panel.server.roomNotFoundDesc: Private rooms must be created first
panel.server.tooManyObjectsDesc: To connect to the server, you must have fewer than {{count}} objects
panel.server.tooManyObjectsDesc200: To connect to the server, you must have fewer than 200 objects
panel.server.tooManyObjectsTitle: Too many objects on your stage
panel.settings.animCommandTutorEditorDes: To add commands to your animations, you need to go into Animator mode:
panel.settings.animCommandTutorInfo:
What is Command Animation?
These are additional commands you can add to your datapack that should be called during animation. For example, make a sound or call a particle or even change your gamemode. Anything!

To add the commands you want, move the timeline at the bottom of the program to the desired point in time, then using the "Command Animation" window on the side, click "Add command" and specify the command you want.

If necessary, you can add more commands, as well as change their order of execution by clicking on the arrows to the left of the commands.

Once exported to datapack, your commands will be called at the point you selected on the timeline.
Good luck!
panel.settings.animHeadTutorAnimatorDesc: In order to prepare the heads for animations, you need to go back to the editor mode:
panel.settings.animHeadTutorInfo:
1. First, you need to open HeadPaint to draw the head texture you want.
You can open it using the button in the top panel, with the logo , or you can find the HeadPaint button right in the head properties.

2. Once you've drawn a texture, you need to turn it into a TextureValue, which is the special code that Minecraft uses to display the texture in the game.
To do this, use the "Create ready-made heads" button at the very bottom of HeadPaint.

3. When the processing has finished, open your head properties and under "TextureValue" you will see the code in the "Current" field. Click on "Add to List" and this way you will save the ready head for future animation.

4. Do these operations again to make all the necessary key textures for your head.

5. Now you can head into Animator mode!
In the Saved TextureValue list, where you added the desired textures, you will see the "Set as current" button - use it to set the desired texture on the head and thus you will create the keyframe of your animation.
Good luck!
panel.settings.assetsExportDesc: You can export your entire Assets to use as a backup or send it to another computer.
panel.settings.assetsImportDesc: You can import your Assets that you saved previously.
panel.settings.combinationAlreadyInUse: This combination is already in use
panel.settings.customizeHotkeys: You can customize the hotkeys however you like
panel.settings.historyDesc: Here you can view the complete history of up to 100 of your saves, including those that failed
panel.settings.installPluginDesc: Click to choose or drag the .js file into BDEngine to install the plugin.
panel.settings.learnCombiningProjectsDes:
The first way:
Open the first project. Then drag the second project file into the editor window with the mouse. And select Merge projects.
Second method:
Open the first project, select everything with {ctrl+A}, copy with {ctrl+C}, then open the second project and paste the contents of the first with {ctrl+V}.
panel.settings.learnCreateAnimationDesc:
Animations are created using groups of objects. You need to divide your model in the Editor mode into the desired groups that you want to animate. You can then go to the Animator to animate the movement, rotation, or size of your model groups.
To change the point at which the group is rotated, see Move Pivot below.
panel.settings.learnEmptyKeyDesc:
Clicking creates a key for the selected group.
Pressing together with {shift} creates keys for nested groups as well.
Pressing together with {ctrl+shift} will create keys for all groups on the scene as well.
panel.settings.learnImportCommandDesc: Copy the required command and then paste it using {ctrl+V}.
panel.settings.learnMovePivotDesc:
You can move Pivot to the desired part of the model, group or selected objects by holding {alt} and moving the controller (Move, Rotate, Size).
This is useful if you want to rotate the model relative to a special point.
Note that the new Pivot position is only saved for groups.
panel.settings.learnRectangularSelection: Hold down {ctrl} and draw a rectangle with the mouse to select objects using rectangular selection.
panel.settings.learnViewSceneStatsDesc: In order to find out how many Block Displays, Item Displays, Text Displays you have on the stage, as well as the number of groups, tris and the number of each type of block, item - you can call the statistics window by clicking on {Tab}.
panel.settings.logsDesc: Logs | Press F3 to download detailed logs and  project
panel.settings.pluginsModifiedRestartDes: Plugins have been modified. Restart BDEngine to apply the changes.
panel.settings.problemDescription: Detailed description of the problem, reproduction steps
panel.settings.reportAgreement: I agree that editor logs and the current active model/animation project will be sent with the report.
panel.settings.resetAllDesc: This will reset all editor settings, as well as editor content, ASSETS, PLUGINS, HISTORY OF SAVES, cache, and some cookies.
panel.settings.resetContentDesc: If you observe strange errors with models or textures, try resetting the editor content so it can re-install itself.
panel.settings.resettingPaintedTexturesI: If your heads contain both a TextureValue and a Painted label and you want to keep the TextureValue by removing the painted texture, use this feature:

window.command.commandBlockDesc: Copy the commands below and paste them inside a command block in Minecraft. Execute them one by one. You can also export the model as a datapack!
window.command.datapackDesc: Download the finished Datapack and use the commands below to summon the model and then activate its animation!
window.command.devAttention: ATTENTION: THE FOLDER YOU HAVE SELECTED WILL BE CLEARED.
window.command.devDesc:
Create and specify a folder for the DEV datapack, inside the datapacks folder of your world.
When exporting, the contents of the folder will be updated without downloading the zip datapack.
window.command.errorNBTDesc: Check the list of objects scene. Objects with an error in the NBT field are marked in red. Select them, and then you will see a red information label in the properties.
window.command.errorNBTTitle: You have incorrect NBT fields in objects and/or groups
window.command.headTextureDesc:
Note the heads labeled [Painted]. They need to be converted into ready-made heads using HeadPaint.
1. In HeadPaint, scroll down to the "Bake the textures" section.
2. Click the "Create ready-made heads" button to automatically process the heads.

If the button is inactive, you will find instructions above it on how to obtain the API key required for this feature to work.
window.command.noObjectsDesc: Create your first object, or maybe an entire model, and then come back here again!
window.command.removeModelInfo: To remove a model, stand nearby and select the desired distance to avoid affecting other models.
window.command.warningCameraAnimationCho: Warning: Camera animation may be choppy. Smooth camera is only available in version 1.21.2 and later.
window.command.warningCameraAnimationOnl: Warning: Camera animation is only available in version 1.20.2 and later.
window.command.warningHitboxes1205:
Warning: Hitboxes will be completely invisible only in version 1.21.2 and later.
Hitbox sizes can be adjusted in version 1.20.5 and later.
window.command.warningHitboxes1212: Warning: Hitboxes will be completely invisible only in version 1.21.2 and later.

window.introduction.animateHeadsDesc: Change the TextureValue on the heads to create the animation.
window.introduction.animationDesc:
Create animations for your models.
Works based on vanilla commands.
Export to DataPack.
window.introduction.anySoundsFromGameDes:
All sounds that are in Minecraft and available via /playsound
are now under your control.
window.introduction.cameraTrackingIRLDes: Connect your phone as a virtual camera to record live camera movements.
window.introduction.createCutScenesDesc: Animate your camera to create cut scenes for your projects!
window.introduction.createUsePublishDesc:
Create a new one.
Use the work of other authors.
Share your own work.
window.introduction.customPivotDesc: Change Pivot groups to change the center of rotation. You must press Alt and start moving.
window.introduction.disclaimer: Not official Minecraft products. Not approved by or associated with Mojang.
window.introduction.discordCommunityDesc: Find like-minded people, find help, keep up with the news
window.introduction.drawOnHeadsDesc: You can paint, fill, erase on multiple heads at once at the same time.
window.introduction.findBDEDesc1:
Provide a link where you came from
such as a YouTube video or Tiktok.
Maybe you found out about it on social media?
window.introduction.findBDEDesc2: Any link where you learned about BDEngine.
window.introduction.findBDEDesc3: I'm very pleased when the project is talked about anywhere, so it would be really cool if you could leave a link so I can take a look!
window.introduction.findBDEPlease: Share the link with me please! If you don't want to or have found the project yourself, click Next
window.introduction.flipDesc: You can mirror selected objects via Context menu > Flip Selection.
window.introduction.groupDesc: Group objects for quick positioning as well as resizing.
window.introduction.headFinderDesc: Huge catalog of finished heads, broken down into categories and tags.
window.introduction.headPaintDesc:
Create unique models from heads.
Create blocks, characters, whatever you want.
All the same without mods and resource packs.
window.introduction.headSizesDesc: Preset head sizes for different scenarios that require a certain number of pixels.
window.introduction.modelCatalogDesc: Here you will find a huge variety of models and animations in different categories
window.introduction.modelDesc:
Create models from existing Minecraft blocks and items!
Does not require mods or resource packs.
Export in Minecraft Commands format.
window.introduction.physicsSimulationDes: Physics simulation for baking into animation, with configurable collision, friction, bounce, and kinematic damping properties.
window.introduction.pianoRollDesc:
Create melodies from game sounds using Piano Roll
the way you do in FL Studio and similar programs.
window.introduction.soundDesc:
Create soundtracks
for your animations or projects
Works via /playsound
window.introduction.telegramChannelDesc: News of this project and more.
window.introduction.textureToModelDesc:
Get a ready-made character model from heads, based on a player's skin or nickname.
Create a ready-made custom block of heads using the necessary textures
Transform an image up to 128x into a huge canvas of heads.
